sections.paragraph: ἀεί [later form of αἰεί. IE, cp. αἰών] adv. with focus on regularity, customarily / from time to time / always: make a habit of resisting the Holy Spirit Ac 7:51; constantly, without fail 2 Cor 4:11; Cretans perpetually tell lies Tit 1:12. In 2 Pet 1:12 the point is that the writer will miss no opportunity.
